title: PE-Term-SGA, rep1
treatment_protocol_ch1: Four tissue biopsies (one sample/quadrant, excluding the chorionic plate) are collected per placenta, rinsed in PBS to remove contaminating maternal blood, pooled, crushed into a powder, and snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen.
extract_protocol_ch1: To control for batch affect, samples were randomized into groups of 20 for processing. A 100 mg aliquot was used to prepare an mRNA extract using Trizol followed by RNAeasy spin columns. RNA purity and integrity were determined using 28S to 18S rRNA ratio (Agilent Bioanalyzer).
label_protocol_ch1: Biotinylated cDNA was prepared by the Princess Margaret Genomics Centre (Toronto, Canada) as per Affymetrics protocols.
hyb_protocol: Hybridization was performed by the Princess Margaret Genomics Centre (Toronto, Canada) as per Affymetrics protocols.
scan_protocol: Scanning was performed by the Princess Margaret Genomics Centre (Toronto, Canada) as per Affymetrics protocols.
description: SAMPLE_10021,Placenta sample purchased from the Research Centre for Women’s and Infants’ Health (RCWIH) BioBank (Toronto, Canada), which utilizes a systematic sampling procedure in which four tissue biopsies (one sample/quadrant) are collected and pooled per placenta. At the time of sample collection and purchase, PE was defined as the onset of systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or diastolic pressure ≥ 90 mmHg after the 20th week of gestation, accompanied by proteinuria (greater than 300 mg protein/day, or greater ≥ 2+ by dipstick). CH: chronic maternal hypertension; systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or sustained diastolic ≥ 90 mmHg before the 20th week of gestation. Preterm: delivery prior to 34 weeks gestation. SGA: small-for-gestational-age infant; neonatal birth weight < 10th percentile for gestational age and sex. AGA: average-for-gestational age infant.
data_processing: Data was processed, normalized, and converted into log2 values in R 3.0.1 using the Affy library from Bioconductor.,The file complete_dataset.csv contains the expression information for 14,651 genes found in common across the different array platforms analyzed. Row names contain gene symbols.

At the time of sample collection and purchase, PE was defined as the onset of systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or diastolic pressure ≥ 90 mmHg after the 20th week of gestation, accompanied by proteinuria (greater than 300 mg protein/day, or greater ≥ 2+ by dipstick). CH: chronic maternal hypertension; systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or sustained diastolic ≥ 90 mmHg before the 20th week of gestation. Preterm: delivery prior to 34 weeks gestation. SGA: small-for-gestational-age infant; neonatal birth weight < 10th percentile for gestational age and sex. AGA: average-for-gestational age infant.

At the time of sample collection and purchase, PE was defined as the onset of systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or diastolic pressure ≥ 90 mmHg after the 20th week of gestation, accompanied by proteinuria (greater than 300 mg protein/day, or greater ≥ 2+ by dipstick). CH: chronic maternal hypertension; systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or sustained diastolic ≥ 90 mmHg before the 20th week of gestation. Preterm: delivery prior to 34 weeks gestation. SGA: small-for-gestational-age infant; neonatal birth weight < 10th percentile for gestational age and sex. AGA: average-for-gestational age infant.

extract_protocol_ch1: To control for batch affect, samples were randomized into groups of 20 for processing. A 100 mg aliquot was used to prepare an mRNA extract using Trizol followed by RNAeasy spin columns. RNA purity and integrity were determined using 28S to 18S rRNA ratio (Agilent Bioanalyzer).
label_protocol_ch1: Biotinylated cDNA was prepared by the Princess Margaret Genomics Centre (Toronto, Canada) as per Affymetrics protocols.
hyb_protocol: Hybridization was performed by the Princess Margaret Genomics Centre (Toronto, Canada) as per Affymetrics protocols.
scan_protocol: Scanning was performed by the Princess Margaret Genomics Centre (Toronto, Canada) as per Affymetrics protocols.
description: SAMPLE_8083,Placenta sample purchased from the Research Centre for Women’s and Infants’ Health (RCWIH) BioBank (Toronto, Canada), which utilizes a systematic sampling procedure in which four tissue biopsies (one sample/quadrant) are collected and pooled per placenta. At the time of sample collection and purchase, PE was defined as the onset of systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or diastolic pressure ≥ 90 mmHg after the 20th week of gestation, accompanied by proteinuria (greater than 300 mg protein/day, or greater ≥ 2+ by dipstick). CH: chronic maternal hypertension; systolic pressure ≥ 140 mmHg and/or sustained diastolic ≥ 90 mmHg before the 20th week of gestation. Preterm: delivery prior to 34 weeks gestation. SGA: small-for-gestational-age infant; neonatal birth weight < 10th percentile for gestational age and sex. AGA: average-for-gestational age infant.
data_processing: Data was processed, normalized, and converted into log2 values in R 3.0.1 using the Affy library from Bioconductor.,The file complete_dataset.csv contains the expression information for 14,651 genes found in common across the different array platforms analyzed. Row names contain gene symbols.
